scenario.context: 50 employees, B2B fintech, AWS + GitHub + Okta + Datadog stack, no SOC 2 yet, targeting Type II in 6 months.

reveals.html:
SOC 2 Type II Readiness Report
Quill Finance · 50 employees · Target: Type II audit in 6 months
58%
Met
9
Partial
14
Not Met
8
N/A
4

Quill Finance has the foundation (Okta + Datadog + GitHub) but is missing formal processes and several access controls. Estimated 4–6 months of focused work to reach audit-ready state.

Critical gaps requiring immediate attention
P0
CC6.5 — Access deprovisioning is manual and inconsistent
Okta is in place but offboarding requires 4 manual steps across IT, HR, and Engineering. We found 3 terminated employees with active GitHub access in the last 90 days.
Effort: 24h · Cost: $0–$2k · Tools: Okta Workflows + offboarding runbook
Fix in 2 weeks →
P0
CC7.2 — No formal incident response plan
Datadog alerts exist but there's no documented IRP, no on-call rotation for security incidents, and no tabletop exercises run. Auditors will fail this control immediately.
Effort: 32h · Cost: $0–$5k · Tools: PagerDuty + IRP template (use AgentAegis policy_generate)
Generate plan →
P0
CC9.1 — No documented vulnerability management program
No regular vuln scanning, no patch SLAs, no risk register. Required for Type II.
Effort: 40h · Cost: $5k–$12k · Tools: Snyk or AgentAegis vuln scanning + policy
Roadmap →
P1
CC1.1 — No code of conduct or ethics training
No security awareness training program. KnowBe4 or similar required. Annual training records needed for audit.
Effort: 16h · Cost: $1k–$3k/yr · Tools: KnowBe4 or Hoxhunt
Quick win →
P1
CC3.1 — No annual risk assessment on file
No formal risk register, no documented risk tolerance, no BIA. Requires a structured assessment, not just a vuln scan.
Effort: 40h · Cost: $0–$8k · Tools: Vanta/Drata workflow OR consultant
Roadmap →
P2
A1.2 — Backup procedures undocumented
RDS automated backups are running but no documented RPO/RTO targets, no quarterly restore tests on file.
Effort: 16h · Cost: $0 · Tools: existing AWS Backup + runbook
Document →

90-day roadmap
Sequenced by dependency · 3 quick wins first
WeekActionEffortCost
1–2Quick wins: document offboarding runbook, generate IRP, enable security awareness training~72h$2k
3–6Implement vuln management program (CC9.1), formalize change management (CC8.1)~80h$8k
7–9Annual risk assessment (CC3.1), policy framework (8 policies via policy_generate)~96h$8k
10–12Backup/DR testing, vendor management process, board reporting cadence~64h$4k
13+Type II observation period begins. Engage auditor (Q3).—$15k–$25k

Estimated readiness in 90 days: 87% (up from 58%). Total cost: $22k–$47k including audit.

What your agent did
// Agent: "Run a SOC 2 readiness assessment on Quill Finance and give us a roadmap" const assessment = await mcp.call("compliance_framework_check", { framework: "soc2", organization_profile: { industry: "fintech", employee_count: 50, handles_pii: true, handles_payment_cards: true, cloud_providers: ["aws"], has_soc_report: false, has_pentest: true, has_security_team: true, tools_in_use: ["okta", "datadog", "github", "aws backup"] } }); const roadmap = await mcp.call("control_gap_analysis", { framework: "soc2", failing_controls: assessment.evaluations.filter(e => e.status !== "met"), budget_constraint: "medium", timeline_constraint: "6_months" }); // Total cost: $1.00 (framework_check $0.50 + gap_analysis $0.50)
